questions.question: Which CSS property prevents image distortion when using a fixed height in a gallery?
questions.explanation: object-fit: cover ensures the image fills the container while maintaining its aspect ratio, cropping excess instead of stretching.

questions.question: What Grid value creates responsive columns without media queries?
questions.explanation: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(200px, 1fr)) creates as many columns as fit, each at least 200px, automatically adjusting without media queries.

questions.question: What attribute enables native lazy loading for gallery images?
questions.explanation: loading='lazy' defers loading of off-screen images until the user scrolls near them, improving initial page load performance.

questions.question: Which CSS property should you use for smooth hover animations in a gallery?
questions.explanation: The transition property enables smooth interpolation between states on hover, such as transform: scale() for zoom effects.

questions.question: What is the accessibility requirement for gallery images?
options: All images must have alt text
All images must be lazy loaded
All images must be the same size
questions.explanation: Every image must have descriptive alt text for screen readers. This is a fundamental accessibility and HTML specification requirement.

questions.question: What is the difference between auto-fill and auto-fit in CSS Grid for galleries?
options: auto-fill keeps empty tracks; auto-fit collapses them
auto-fit keeps empty tracks; auto-fill collapses them
questions.explanation: auto-fill creates grid tracks even if empty, while auto-fit collapses empty tracks. auto-fit is usually preferred for galleries.

questions.question: Which CSS property creates a masonry-style layout?
questions.explanation: CSS columns (column-count) create a masonry-like layout where items flow top-to-bottom in columns. CSS Grid masonry is not yet widely supported.

questions.question: What should you set on image containers when using transform: scale() for hover zoom?
questions.explanation: overflow: hidden on the container prevents the zoomed image from overflowing its boundaries, keeping the layout clean.

questions.question: Which element is best for semantically grouping an image with its caption?
questions.explanation: The <figure> element with <figcaption> provides semantic grouping of images with their captions, improving accessibility and SEO.

questions.question: What CSS property prevents cumulative layout shift (CLS) in image galleries?
questions.explanation: aspect-ratio reserves the correct amount of vertical space before the image loads, preventing layout shifts that cause Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).

questions.question: How can you make gallery images focusable for keyboard navigation?
questions.explanation: Adding tabindex='0' makes non-interactive elements like images focusable via keyboard, allowing keyboard users to navigate through gallery items.

questions.question: What image format provides the best compression for web galleries?
questions.explanation: WebP offers 25-35% smaller file sizes than JPEG with similar quality. Using WebP with a JPEG fallback ensures broad browser compatibility.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the decoding='async' attribute on gallery images?
options: Decodes the image asynchronously to reduce main thread work
Delays image decoding to after page load
questions.explanation: decoding='async' allows the browser to decode the image off the main thread, reducing jank and improving perceived performance.

questions.question: Which property creates a caption overlay that slides up from the bottom on hover?
questions.explanation: translateY(100%) positions the caption below the image, transitioning to translateY(0) on hover to slide it up into view.

questions.question: What is a common issue with using CSS columns for masonry galleries?
options: Items are ordered top-to-bottom, not left-to-right
questions.explanation: CSS columns fill items top-to-bottom in each column before moving to the next, which may not match the expected left-to-right reading order.

questions.question: What attribute provides responsive images at different viewport sizes?
questions.explanation: srcset lists different image file URLs for different resolutions, and sizes tells the browser which size to use at different viewport widths.

questions.question: Which CSS property applies a darkening effect to gallery images on hover?
questions.explanation: filter: brightness(0.7) darkens the image on hover, often combined with a caption overlay for better text readability.

questions.question: What gap value is recommended for grid galleries?
questions.explanation: A gap of 8-16px provides sufficient spacing between gallery items without wasting too much space, balancing aesthetics and content density.

questions.question: What does content-visibility: auto do for gallery performance?
options: Defers rendering of off-screen gallery sections
questions.explanation: content-visibility: auto defers rendering of off-screen sections until they are near the viewport, significantly improving initial page load performance.

questions.question: How should hover-only effects be handled for accessibility in image galleries?
questions.explanation: Hover effects must also be triggered by :focus so keyboard users receive the same visual feedback when tabbing through gallery items.
